article_meta.title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11 Feb 2026 Kid Rock buried Puerto Rican rapper Bad Bunny in the gossip site TMZ's poll asking fans their feelings about the 2026 Super Bowl Halftime show, with Rock nearly doubling Bad Bunny's support. The poll may not have gone exactly how TMZ expected as Kid Rock soared ahead of Bad Bunny with 64.5 percent saying Rock had the better show to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ shut the poll down. TMZ tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny by characterizing the rabidly anti-Trump rapper's show as taking place on "one of the world's biggest stages," while merely saying that Kid Rock "tried to outshine" Bad Bunny. The site also frontloaded the poll by putting Bad Bunny's name first, writing, "Who had the better halftime show — Bad Bunny or Kid Rock?" Fans did not fall for the push… Many fans jumped on TMZ's X post to joke that the poll probably wasn't going in the direction the gossip site thought it would. According to some of the latest statistics, it seems that nearly half of Super Bowl viewers tuned out of the league's Bad Bunny Halftime Show. Finally, in a strange turn of events, rapper Bad Bunny wiped all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance. Follow Warner Todd Huston on Facebook at: Facebook.com/Warner.Todd.Huston, Truth Social @WarnerToddHuston, or at X/Twitter @WTHuston EntertainmentPoliticsSportsBad BunnyKid RockSuper BowlTMZTurning Point USA
---

```json
{
  "summary": "Kid Rock significantly outperformed Bad Bunny in a TMZ poll asking fans about competing halftime shows during the 2026 Super Bowl period. Rock received 64.5 percent support compared to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ closed the poll. The article suggests TMZ attempted to favor Bad Bunny by describing his performance as occurring on \"one of the world's biggest stages\" while stating Kid Rock merely \"tried to outshine\" him, and by listing Bad Bunny's name first in the poll question. Despite these efforts, fans overwhelmingly preferred Kid Rock's show. The piece notes that approximately half of Super Bowl viewers reportedly tuned out during Bad Bunny's official halftime performance, and mentions that Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately following his Super Bowl appearance. The article characterizes Bad Bunny as a \"rabidly anti-Trump rapper.\"",
  "key_facts": [
    "Kid Rock received 64.5% support versus Bad Bunny's 35.5% in TMZ's halftime show poll before it was shut down",
    "TMZ allegedly structured the poll to favor Bad Bunny by using positive framing and listing his name first",
    "Nearly half of Super Bowl viewers reportedly tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime performance",
    "Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance",
    "The article describes Bad Bunny as a \"rabidly anti-Trump rapper\""
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Breitbart Fact Check"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Kid Rock significantly outperformed Bad Bunny in a TMZ poll asking fans about competing halftime shows during the 2026 Super Bowl period. Rock received 64.5 percent support compared to Bad Bunny's 35.5 percent before TMZ closed the poll. The article suggests TMZ attempted to favor Bad Bunny by describing his performance as occurring on "one of the world's biggest stages" while stating Kid Rock merely "tried to outshine" him, and by listing Bad Bunny's name first in the poll question. Despite these efforts, fans overwhelmingly preferred Kid Rock's show. The piece notes that approximately half of Super Bowl viewers reportedly tuned out during Bad Bunny's official halftime performance, and mentions that Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately following his Super Bowl appearance. The article characterizes Bad Bunny as a "rabidly anti-Trump rapper."

TMZ allegedly structured the poll to favor Bad Bunny by using positive framing and listing his name first
Nearly half of Super Bowl viewers reportedly tuned out during Bad Bunny's halftime performance
Bad Bunny deleted all his social media accounts immediately after his Super Bowl performance
The article describes Bad Bunny as a "rabidly anti-Trump rapper"
